description_long: This is a vintage, weathered lantern with a cylindrical metal body and a rounded, bulbous glass enclosure at its center. The metal frame is heavily rusted, showing a mix of teal paint and exposed brownish-orange corrosion, giving it an aged, industrial look. Two vertical side supports connect the base to the top, with a wire guard wrapping around the glass to protect it. The top features a vented cap with a small handle, while the base is wide and stable, likely designed to sit securely on flat surfaces. A thin, curved wire handle arches over the lantern, allowing it to be carried or hung. The glass enclosure is clear and slightly frosted, typical of oil or kerosene lanterns used in outdoor or utilitarian urban settings.
description: A weathered, teal and rusted metal lantern with a rounded glass enclosure and protective wire guard, featuring a curved carrying handle and a wide, stable base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full glass enclosure, protective wire guard, and the main control knob at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved wire handle and the profile of the vented top cap.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the glass enclosure and the symmetrical metal supports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the wire handle and side profile of the lantern's body and base.
